---
title: "CPS Spatial Distribution Plot"
subtitle: "Schools by Community Areas & Enrollment Threshold Highlighting"
author:
  - name: "Andi Fang"
  - name: "Minyang Xu"
date: "2026-02-25"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
        \fvset{breaklines=true,breakanywhere=true}
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Introduction

This document visualizes Chicago Public Schools (CPS) on top of Chicago community boundaries.
Schools are split into two groups using an enrollment threshold (the 0.75 quantile of total enrollment), and plotted with different marker colors.

- **Blue points**: schools at/below the 0.75 quantile threshold  
- **Red points**: schools above the 0.75 quantile threshold  

---

# Environment Setup

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely import wkt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import os
```

We define file paths relative to the project root to ensure reproducibility.

```{python}
project_root = Path(os.environ.get("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", Path.cwd())).resolve().parent

raw_community = project_root / "data" / "raw-data" / "community_boundaries.csv"
raw_schools   = project_root / "data" / "derived-data" / "cps_df.csv"
```

# Load Data
```{python}
comm_df = pd.read_csv(raw_community, sep=";")
schools_df = pd.read_csv(raw_schools, sep=None, engine="python")
```

# Convert to GeoDataFrames
```{python}
comm_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    comm_df,
    geometry=comm_df["the_geom"].apply(wkt.loads),
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

schools_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    schools_df,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(schools_df["School_Longitude"], schools_df["School_Latitude"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)
```


# Data Cleaning
```{python}
schools_df["School_Longitude"] = pd.to_numeric(schools_df["School_Longitude"], errors="coerce")
schools_df["School_Latitude"]  = pd.to_numeric(schools_df["School_Latitude"],  errors="coerce")
schools_df = schools_df.dropna(subset=["School_Longitude", "School_Latitude"]).copy()

schools_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    schools_df,
    geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(schools_df["School_Longitude"], schools_df["School_Latitude"]),
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

schools_gdf["Total"] = (
    schools_gdf["Total"]
      .astype(str)
      .str.replace(",", "", regex=False)
)
schools_gdf["Total"] = pd.to_numeric(schools_gdf["Total"], errors="coerce")

schools_gdf[["Total"]].describe()
```


# Threshold Definition

We define the threshold as the 0.75 quantile of Total enrollment.

```{python}
q = 0.75
thr = schools_gdf["Total"].quantile(q)

schools_gdf["above_thr"] = schools_gdf["Total"] > thr
schools_gdf["above_thr"].value_counts(dropna=False)

schools_gdf["size_bin"] = pd.qcut(
    schools_gdf["Total"],
    q=4,
    duplicates="drop"
)

size_map = {b: s for b, s in zip(schools_gdf["size_bin"].cat.categories, [10, 25, 45, 70])}
schools_gdf["ms"] = schools_gdf["size_bin"].map(size_map)

schools_gdf[["Total", "size_bin", "ms"]].head()
```


# Plot: Schools over Community Areas
```{python}
COMM_FILL = "#dbeff2"
COMM_EDGE = "#111111"

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 10))

# community polygons
comm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    facecolor="white",
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=1.6,
    zorder=1
)

# schools: below threshold (blue)
schools_gdf.loc[~schools_gdf["above_thr"]].plot(
    ax=ax,
    markersize=15,
    color="#2b6cb0",
    alpha=0.75,
    linewidth=0,
    label="Schools ≤ threshold",
    zorder=2
)

# schools: above threshold (red)
schools_gdf.loc[schools_gdf["above_thr"]].plot(
    ax=ax,
    markersize=25,
    color="#d62828",
    alpha=0.85,
    linewidth=0,
    label="Schools > threshold",
    zorder=3
)

ax.set_title("Chicago Public Schools by Community Areas", fontsize=16, pad=14)
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.legend(
    title="Student Enrollment (75th Percentile Split)",
    labels=[
        "Enrollment ≤ 75th Percentile",
        "Enrollment > 75th Percentile"
    ],
    loc="lower left",
    frameon=True,
    fontsize=12,
    title_fontsize=13
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


# Exporting High-Resolution PNG

For submission purposes, we export the figure as a high-resolution PNG file.

```{python}
output_path = project_root / "data" / "derived-data" / "plot_app_spatial_scatter.png"

fig.savefig(output_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print(f"Saved plot as PNG: {output_path}")
```
